skill.name: kling-3-0
skill.description: Kling 3.0 video generation on RunComfy. Kling 3.0 (also called Kling V3.0) is Kuaishou Technology's third-generation multi-shot video model with native synchronized audio and consistent character identity across shots. This skill covers all six Kling 3.0 endpoints, spanning three rendering tiers (Standard, Pro, 4K) and two modes (text-to-video, image-to-video). Calls runcomfy run kling/kling-3.0/<tier>/<mode> through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "kling", "kling 3.0", "kling v3", "kling pro", "kling 4k", "kling text to video", "kling image to video", or any explicit ask to generate or animate with Kling 3.0.
skill.summary: Generate cinematic multi-shot videos with Kling 3.0 across Standard, Pro, and 4K tiers, with optional synchronized audio.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: All 99 static findings are false positives. The SKILL.md is pure Markdown documentation for an AI assistant to learn when and how to invoke the RunComfy CLI for Kling 3.0 video generation. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' alerts are Markdown code-block formatting. 'Hardcoded URLs' point to the legitimate runcomfy.com service. 'Path traversal' triggers on `<absolute/path>` placeholders. 'Hidden file access' references the standard `~/.config/runcomfy/token.json` XDG config path. No executable code exists; no injection vectors are present. The file includes an explicit Security & Privacy section documenting its safe design.

low_findings.title: CLI command documentation flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 68 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' in SKILL.md. These are Markdown code-block backticks and documentation examples of the `runcomfy run` CLI command. The file contains no executable code; it is pure Markdown documentation intended to teach an AI agent how to invoke the CLI. The actual shell invocation is performed by the user via the RunComfy CLI, not by this skill. No injection vector exists.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 15 instances of 'Hardcoded URL'. All URLs point to the legitimate RunComfy service (runcomfy.com, docs.runcomfy.com) or are example placeholder URLs for image inputs in the `--input` parameter. This is expected behavior for a skill that documents a hosted video generation API.

low_findings.title: Path placeholders flagged as path traversal
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'path traversal sequence' on the `<absolute/path>` placeholder in the `--output-dir` parameter documentation. This is a standard documentation convention to show the user where to substitute their own output directory. No actual path traversal occurs.

low_findings.title: Standard XDG config path flagged as hidden file access
low_findings.description: The Security & Privacy section documents the standard `~/.config/runcomfy/token.json` path used by the RunComfy CLI for token storage. This is a standard XDG Base Directory config path on Linux/macOS. The file is created with mode 0600 (user-read-only) by the `runcomfy login` flow, which is the correct secure default for API credential storage.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash command examples in documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: The file contains six Bash command examples showing how to invoke each Kling 3.0 endpoint via the RunComfy CLI. These are inline code blocks in Markdown, not executable scripts. Each uses JSON via `--input` flag which is passed as a parameter, not shell-interpreted, preventing any shell-injection surface.

content.value_statement: AI agents need a reliable way to call advanced video generation models without writing custom API code. This skill gives Claude and Codex direct access to all six Kling 3.0 endpoints on RunComfy, from draft previews to 4K masters with native audio.
actual_capabilities: Generates text-to-video clips across three Kling 3.0 quality tiers: Standard, Pro, and native 4K
Animates still images via image-to-video mode with optional tail-image for frame-locked endings
Produces multi-shot sequences in a single generation with consistent character identity across shots
Creates native synchronized audio in the same generation pass (dialogue, ambient, music)
Supports aspect ratios 16:9, 9:16, 1:1, 4:3, 3:4, and 21:9 with durations from 3 to 15 seconds
Downloads generated video files directly to a user-specified output directory via the RunComfy CLI
limitations: Maximum 15 seconds per generation across all three tiers
Image-to-video requires a publicly fetchable HTTPS image URL; local files are not supported
4K output files are large and may require planning for disk space and bandwidth
Generation cost scales with duration and tier; 4K with audio costs roughly 5x Standard

use_cases.title: Social media content creator
use_cases.description: Quickly generate vertical 9:16 video clips for TikTok, Reels, and Shorts using the Standard tier for fast, cheap iteration.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content creators

use_cases.title: Brand creative team
use_cases.description: Produce 1080p hero-quality brand stories with consistent characters and synchronized dialogue using the Pro tier with audio enabled.

use_cases.title: Film and post-production studio
use_cases.description: Render native 4K finished masters at flat-rate pricing for big-screen cinematic projects where resolution matters.

prompt_templates.title: Basic text-to-video (beginner)
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 5-second 16:9 video using the Kling 3.0 Standard text-to-video endpoint. Prompt: "A golden retriever running on a sunny beach, slow motion, cinematic."

prompt_templates.title: Image-to-video animation (intermediate)
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate the provided portrait image using Kling 3.0 Standard image-to-video. Add gentle camera dolly-in, subtle breathing motion, and warm natural light. Duration: 6 seconds, aspect ratio 16:9.
prompt_templates.scenario: Animate a still reference image with motion

prompt_templates.title: Multi-shot cinematic with audio (advanced)
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Kling V3.0 Pro text-to-video with generate_audio: true. Prompt: "Cinematic multi-shot of a young American couple celebrating their anniversary at a candlelit rooftop restaurant. Shot 1: wide of the city skyline at golden hour. Shot 2: medium two-shot, the couple toasting. Shot 3: tight on the woman's smile, soft bokeh, warm fill light. Subtle ambient string music, gentle wind, distant traffic." Duration: 10 seconds, aspect ratio 21:9.
prompt_templates.scenario: Hero-quality brand content with synchronized audio

prompt_templates.title: 4K finished master (advanced)
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Kling V3.0 4K text-to-video with generate_audio: true for a flat-rate 4K master. Prompt: "Aerial drone shot descending through morning mist over a dense pine forest valley. Camera slowly pushes through clouds to reveal a sunlit river below. Ambient forest sounds, birdsong, distant water." Duration: 12 seconds, aspect ratio 21:9.
prompt_templates.scenario: Native 4K cinematic output for final delivery

output_examples.input: Generate a 5-second vertical video of a barista pulling an espresso shot with warm cafe ambience.
output_examples.output: A 9:16 vertical video rendered via Kling 3.0 Standard. The barista in a black apron pulls a single espresso shot, steam rising into morning sun, rich crema slowly forming. Close-up handheld camera, shallow depth of field, warm cafe ambience and the hiss of the steam wand. Downloaded to the specified output directory.

output_examples.input: Animate a portrait photo with subtle camera movement and warm golden-hour lighting.
output_examples.output: A 1080p 16:9 video rendered via Kling V3.0 Pro image-to-video. Gentle camera dolly-in on the subject from the source image. Subtle breathing motion, identity-stable features, soft natural light, shallow depth of field. Background shows warm golden-hour glow with a slow drift of dust motes. No dialogue, only ambient room tone.

output_examples.input: Create a 4K hero shot of a misty forest valley with bird ambience.
output_examples.output: A native 3840x2160 21:9 video rendered via Kling V3.0 4K. Aerial drone shot descending through morning mist over a dense pine forest valley. Camera slowly pushes through clouds to reveal a sunlit river below. Ambient forest sounds, birdsong, distant water. Flat-rate pricing at $0.42/s for the 4K tier.

best_practices: Lead prompts with motion and camera language such as 'wide shot, slow push-in' or 'tracking shot, low angle' for better Kling 3.0 adherence
Validate expensive 4K and Pro generations with a Standard-tier draft first to avoid wasted budget on off-target outputs
For image-to-video, restate identity anchors in the prompt to ensure face, pose, and clothing are preserved during animation
anti_patterns: Do not request durations longer than 15 seconds in a single call; this will return a 422 error and waste a billing cycle
Do not combine conflicting style cues in one prompt such as 'photorealistic anime watercolor' as this confuses the model and degrades output quality
Do not use unsupported aspect ratios; only 16:9, 9:16, 1:1, 4:3, 3:4, and 21:9 are accepted by the API

faq.question: What is the maximum duration of a Kling 3.0 clip?
faq.answer: 15 seconds per generation across all three tiers. For longer narratives, segment the script into multiple calls and stitch the results.

faq.question: How is Kling V3.0 4K priced compared to Standard and Pro?
faq.answer: Kling V3.0 4K is a flat $0.42 per second whether or not audio is enabled. Standard is $0.084/s without audio and Pro is $0.112/s without audio. The 4K tier costs roughly 5x Standard for the resolution upgrade.

faq.question: Does Kling 3.0 support multi-shot in a single generation?
faq.answer: Yes. All Kling 3.0 endpoints accept multi-segment prompts. Number the shots such as 'Shot 1:' and 'Shot 2:' and the model will preserve character identity across them.

faq.question: Can Kling 3.0 generate audio?
faq.answer: Yes. Set generate_audio to true. The model produces synchronized dialogue, ambient sound, and music in the same generation pass. On 4K the price stays flat; on Standard and Pro the rate increases about 50% with audio.

faq.question: What aspect ratios does Kling 3.0 support?
faq.answer: 16:9, 9:16, 1:1, 4:3, 3:4, and 21:9. The 4K tier renders 21:9 as wide cinema crops at native 3840x2160.

faq.question: How is Kling 3.0 different from Kling 2.x?
faq.answer: Kling 3.0 has stronger multi-shot identity preservation, longer max duration (15s vs 10s), native 4K on the 4K tier, and unified multi-prompt segment input across all tiers.
